Title: Enhancement of extraplastidic oil synthesis in Chlamydomonas reinhardtii using a type-2 diacylglycerol acyltransferase with a phosphorus starvation–inducible promoter

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SQD2 promoter used as transgene promoter; phosphorus starvation–inducible
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced after phosphorus deprivation; not up-regulated after nitrogen deprivation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cytosolic lipid droplets for engineered TAG increase
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its promoter enabled phosphorus starvation–dependent transgene expression and thereby enhanced TAG accumulation when driving DGTT4
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SQD2 transcript was strongly induced by phosphorus starvation but not by nitrogen starvation. 2. The SQD2 promoter was used to drive nutrient-responsive transgene expression. 3. SQD2 promoter-driven DGTT4 expression successfully enhanced TAG accumulation under P starvation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR/RT-PCR expression analysis; promoter-transgene overexpression assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: TAG serves as storage oil and biofuel feedstock; native SQDG supports membrane remodeling under phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper SQD2 is a core engineering element rather than the primary metabolic enzyme analyzed for TAG synthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable inducible promoter for algal metabolic engineering specifically under phosphorus starvation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR gene expression analysis; no public accession reported

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Served as a phosphorus starvation-induced marker gene supporting identification of P-responsive expression system; direct TAG effect not tested
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGP3 expression was induced by phosphorus deprivation. 2. Its induction supported the concept that phosphorus starvation activates sulfolipid-related genes. 3. It was not functionally validated as a TAG-enhancing target in this study.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Native role linked to sulfolipid biosynthesis under phosphate limitation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a P-starvation marker/background metabolic gene; no transgenic validation for TAG accumulation in this paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible marker for phosphate-starvation responsive engineering, but direct application not established here
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR gene expression analysis; no public accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CC-400 (transformant background); CC-408 for expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Extraplastidic/cytosolic oil synthesis under phosphorus starvation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; feedstock for algal biodiesel/industrial oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low in TAP medium; slightly induced after phosphorus deprivation; low under nitrogen starvation relative to DGTT1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced strongly in transgenic lines under phosphorus starvation via SQD2 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphorus starvation (TAP–P); comparison with nitrogen starvation and TAP medium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes increased TAG accumulation under phosphorus starvation and shifts TAG fatty acid composition toward higher 18:1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGTT4 overexpression driven by the phosphorus starvation–inducible SQD2 promoter significantly increased TAG accumulation under P deprivation. 2. TAG levels rose about 1.5-fold and 2.5-fold in representative DGTT4-overexpressing lines compared with the empty-vector control. 3. Overexpression increased the proportion of 18:1 and reduced 16:0 in TAG, consistent with DGTT4 preference for 18:1-CoA. 4. DGTT4-enhanced TAG accumulated in cytosolic lipid droplets while thylakoid membranes were retained under P starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in C. reinhardtii; qPCR/RT-PCR; TAG quantification by TLC-GC/FAME; Nile Red staining; transmission electron microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key terminal determinant for enhanced TAG accumulation under phosphorus starvation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR gene expression analysis; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious thylakoid disruption under phosphorus starvation in overexpressors; other growth/yield penalties not established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to enhance algal oil production under phosphorus starvation; suitable for stacking with fatty acid modification enzymes using inducible promoters
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGTT4 overexpression was effective whereas DGTT2 and DGTT3 overexpression were not; previous reports cited indicate DGTT4 has in vitro DGAT activity but weak yeast complementation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: CC-400 (transformant background); CC-408 for expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates diacylglycerol with acyl-CoA to form triacylglycerol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: cytosolic lipid droplets under P starvation context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: carbon and energy storage; feedstock for algal biodiesel/industrial oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Moderate transcript level; largely constant after nitrogen deprivation; transcript abundance increased after phosphorus deprivation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in transformants under phosphorus starvation using SQD2 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphorus starvation; comparison with nitrogen starvation and TAP medium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression did not significantly enhance TAG accumulation relative to control under the tested phosphorus starvation conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGTT2 transcripts were detectable at moderate basal levels. 2. DGTT2 transcript abundance increased after P deprivation. 3. SQD2-driven DGTT2 overexpression failed to increase TAG accumulation compared with controls under P starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qPCR gene expression analysis; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority engineering target than DGTT4 for boosting TAG under phosphorus starvation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as likely preferring 16:0-CoA based on cited previous work.
